trails.name: Maunganui bluff track
trails.description: Climb up and over an ancient volcanic crater for some of the best views in the region - pass through some pristine coastal vegetation dominated by Pohutukawa.

trails.name: HONGI HIKA WALK
trails.description: This short, easy walk passes through regenerating native forest to Kororipo pā - it's a great option for children to explore the heritage of early Maori/European settlement.

trails.name: TAITA BRIDLE TRACK
trails.description: This moderately challenging track leads into the beautiful and rugged Waimā Forest.

trails.name: WEKAWEKA TRACK
trails.description: Follow the gentle incline of an old coach road up the valley and onto the Mataraua Forest plateau.

trails.name: WAIRAKAU STREAM TRACK
trails.description: This track is for experienced trampers. It first climbs over a saddle to an old farm road and then descends through regenerating forest to Wairakau Stream. It ends at Lane Cove Hut.

trails.name: SIX FOOT TRACK
trails.description: This is a walk to Framptons Hut and on to the Waimā Main Range Track.

trails.name: WAOKU COACH RD W/WAY (WAIMA TO TUTAMOE)
Lake Ngatu Track
trails.description: Enjoy an easy walk with the family around Lake Ngatu - a freshwater dune lake and recreation reserve. The lake is popular with water sport enthusiasts, day visitors, and locals.

trails.name: Hauturu Highpoint Track
trails.description: Climb onto a razor back ridge in the Waimā Ranges for sensational views and pristine vegetation.

trails.name: Kerikeri River Track
trails.description: Follow the bank of the Kerikeri River through regenerating forest and discover the remains of an historic power house, and Wharepoke and Rainbow Falls. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Kororipo Pā Historic Walk
trails.description: Walk in the footsteps of chief Hongi Hika and early missionaries to Kororipo Pā – this short, easy walk is suitable for children.

trails.name: Mangamuka Tramping Track
trails.description: The Mangamuka Tramping Track takes you through scenic Raetea Forest, a track made for the adventurous at heart.

trails.name: Maunganui Bluff Track
trails.description: Climb up and over an ancient volcanic crater for some of the best views in the region - pass through some pristine coastal vegetation dominated by Pohutukawa.

trails.name: Motukawanui Island Track
trails.description: Enjoy outstanding views of Mahinepua Peninsula, Matauri Bay and other forested islands of the Cavalli group from this walk along the ridge tops.

trails.name: Mount Tutamoe Track
trails.description: Climb through Kaihu Forest to the top of Mount Tutamoe and you will be rewarded by stunning, panoramic views.

trails.name: Pā Road Walk
trails.description: The short Pā Road Walk links Pā Road to the Kerikeri Basin. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Pukatea Ridge Track
trails.description: This is a well-marked track through established bush with valley views and large kauri trees. This track connects with the Waipapa River Track.

trails.name: Signal Station Track
trails.description: Enjoy spectacular views of the harbour and coastline on this short easy walk that’s suitable for children.

trails.name: St Paul's Rock Track
trails.description: This track climbs up to St Paul's Rock, which stands high above Whangaroa township and provides spectacular views of the Whangaroa Harbour. It's suitable for those with moderate fitness.

trails.name: Taronui Bay Recreation Reserve Track
trails.description: Walking the track will take you through a mixed landscape of pasture and wooded areas to the beautiful white sands of Taronui Bay.

trails.name: Te Matua Ngahere Walk
trails.description: View the second largest living kauri in New Zealand, Te Matua Ngahere - it's a good option for kids.

trails.name: Trounson Kauri Park Loop Track
trails.description: Walk through a spectacular stand of kauri and experience the unique character of this ancient forest by day or night.

trails.name: Waimamaku Coastal Track
trails.description: Discover wide open beaches and rocky headlands on the rugged coastline between the Hokianga Harbour and the majestic Waimamaku River.

trails.name: Waiotemarama Waterfall Walk
trails.description: Walk to the beautiful Waiotemarama Waterfall which cascades 20 m down through the pristine rainforest.

trails.name: Waipapa River Track
trails.description: This scenic two-day tramp follows the Waipapa River through the heart of Puketī Forest. It is only recommended for experienced and well-equipped trampers. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Waoku Coach Road Track
trails.description: Follow the gentle incline of an historic coach road through the heart of the Mataraua Forest.

trails.name: Yakas Walk
trails.description: Closed until further notice to prevent the spread of kauri dieback.

trails.name: Duke's Nose (Kaiaraara Rocks) Track
trails.description: Take in spectacular views of Whangaroa Harbour from Duke's Nose.

trails.name: Kaitaia Walkway
trails.description: Kaitaia Walkway is closed.
